mappings.rationaleAI: Pseudorandom number generators and Monte Carlo simulations are core statistical tools (B) and connect to information theory (F) and numeric computation (G.5).
The P versus NP problem is central to computer science theory of computation (G.2), algorithm analysis (G.1), and has implications for cryptography and artificial intelligence (G.6).
Renaissance mathematics covers the introduction of modern notation (A.4.a), advances in navigation and cartography (A.2.b, A.2.c), and perspective geometry as a geometrical aid (A.2.a).

Artificial intelligence covers neural networks, expert systems, and the Turing test, directly addressing AI as a branch of computer science (G.6) and automata theory's connections to neural nets (D.2).
Cryptography's mathematical foundations including RSA and Diffie-Hellman connect to applications of information theory to cryptography (F.4) and computer science (G).
Alan Turing's universal Turing machine established theoretical foundations of computation (G.2), his work on algorithms and computability (G.1), and his concepts relate to automata theory (D).
Prime numbers' crucial application in modern cryptography and internet security (RSA encryption) connects to applications of information theory and number theory to cryptography (F.4).
Probability theory covers statistical inference principles (B.1), distribution functions (B.1.b), and applications in decision theory connecting to game theory (E.1).

Artificial intelligence from the late 1990s perspective covers computational approaches to consciousness and neural networks, connecting to automata theory (D) and AI as a computer science field (G.6).

Gauss's method of least squares connects to statistical estimation (B.2), his work on celestial mechanics to mechanics of particles (H.1), and electromagnetic theory with Weber to electromagnetic theory (H.5).
Euler's number e, the natural logarithm, and exponential function connect to calculatory aspects of algebra through logarithms (A.4.b).
Chaos theory's study of deterministic systems sensitive to initial conditions connects to control theory (E.4) and fluid mechanics applications (H.2).
Newton's Laws of Motion are the foundation of the mathematical treatment of mechanics of particles and systems (H.1).
Einstein's relativity directly addresses space and time as a four-dimensional continuum (H.6) and Riemannian geometry (H.7) through general relativity's curved spacetime.
Indian mathematics covers the decimal place-value system (A.1) and early computational methods including trigonometric tables, connecting to ancient numerical notations (A.1.b).

mappings.episodeTitle: Maths in the Early Islamic World
mappings.rationaleAI: Islamic mathematics covers the development of Arabic numerals and the decimal system (A.1.c), and al-Khwarizmi's algorithms connecting to algebraic notation (A.4.a).
The unintended applications of pure mathematics illustrate mathematical aspects of physical theories (H), including non-Euclidean geometry in general relativity and group theory in particle physics.
